fault_hypothesis: The condition for clamping the result on line 68 is inverted. The code clamps when `clamp` is `False` and does not clamp when it is `True`, due to the condition being `if not clamp:` instead of `if clamp:`.

constraints.intent: The function only passes for interpolation cases (t in [0,1]) and fails for all extrapolation cases.

spec.expr: ((not clamp) and y__5 == y__1) or (clamp and y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: The value after the conditional block must be unclamped if clamp is false, or within the y-range if clamp is true.
The function only behaves correctly when not extrapolating (i.e., x is between x0 and x1).
